mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following months with PAYEMS growth >150k is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5 percent, the labor market becomes sufficiently tight that wage inflation pressures build, leading the Federal Reserve to preemptively tighten monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE is below 5.0, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a federal funds rate at or below 0.5% creates a goldilocks environment of strong job creation and accommodative financing conditions, amplifying the positive effect on equity prices beyond what either series alone would predict.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS_change * low_FEDFUNDS_dummy) in a linear model of 20-day forward returns has a coefficient not significantly positive at the 10% level, or the incremental R-squared from adding the interaction is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession housing recovery regime, rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence, household formation, and construction sector spending, which feeds into broader economic growth and supports higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During the post-2009 recovery period, the correlation between HOUST levels and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.2, or the signal fails to produce positive returns in more than 60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates robust manufacturing activity and supply-side momentum, which directly translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following months with positive INDPRO growth is negative on average over any 5-year rolling window, or the hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above 0.75 percent, the Federal Reserve is actively withdrawing accommodation, raising the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and compressing valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.75, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative at the 10% level, or more than 40% of such episodes produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm strong consumer spending power and broad-based demand momentum, which jointly boost corporate earnings forecasts and drive equity prices higher over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases AND PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both series increase, the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than in months when only one or neither increases (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation is low (CPIAUCSL stable or declining) and the federal funds rate is near zero, real interest rates are deeply negative, which encourages equity investment over fixed income and pushes stock prices higher over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: low and stable inflation with near-zero rates
failure_condition: During periods identified by the trigger, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of observations, or the Sharpe ratio of the strategy based on this signal is below 0.3.
